item.description:
New

  • Ad and tracker blocking (HaGezi "Light"), on by default in Settings → Privacy. It blocks known ad and tracking domains, but not ads a site serves from its own domain.
  • Reader mode: a toolbar button strips an article to clean, readable text with Mozilla's Readability, on your Mac with no network.
  • Real dark theming: per-service dark mode now uses Dark Reader for proper per-element theming, following the app's Light/Dark appearance.
  • Auto dark mode: optionally dark-theme services that lack one while the app is dark. Override per service with Auto, On, or Off.
  • Hide annoyances: optionally hide cookie notices, newsletter pop-ups, and floating bars (off by default).

Fixed

  • Downloading a file now saves it to your Downloads folder; some downloads, including PDFs from Microsoft Teams, did nothing before.
  • Microsoft Teams opens on its current address, so it no longer shows the "Teams has a new URL" banner.
  • Adding a service, or creating a space, now switches to it right away.
  • A brief banner explains that passkey sign-in isn't available in Chorus.

Added

  • Keyboard navigation for the spaces and services rails. With a rail focused, the arrow keys move the selection, and Option with an arrow reorders the focused item.

Changed

  • In the top-bar and hybrid layouts, a service tab shows its icon alone, with its name on hover, and you can drag the open part of the strip to move the window.
  • The cookie-banner setting now spells out what it does: it accepts consent pop-ups for you, tracking cookies included. Turn it off to answer each site yourself.

Fixed

  • A service's unread badge no longer goes stale after you follow a link that switches to it.
  • The preview shown while a service loads fills the pane instead of cropping or stretching it, and it clears once the page finishes loading.

Fixed

  • Dragging a service or space in a top or hybrid rail now reorders it instead of moving the window.
  • Dragging a space to reorder it drops it exactly where you release it.
  • The spaces rail scrolls, so every space and the add button stay reachable when you have more than fit the window.
  • You can no longer delete your last space. With no spaces left, the window went blank and there was nowhere to add a service.
  • After a service you had open is removed, the app opens on a valid service instead of a blank pane.
  • Sign-in works when a service sends you to its login page. Google, Microsoft, Apple, and Yahoo sign-in pages stay in the app instead of opening your browser.
  • A sign-in window no longer gets replaced by an error page, or reloaded to the wrong address, when a network request fails briefly.
  • Web notifications come only from the service that owns the page. Embedded third-party frames can no longer post them in its name.
  • A service that reports a bad unread count can no longer hide the badges of your other services.
  • A service running a call inside an embedded frame is no longer suspended mid-call.
  • The quick switcher updates its list when you rename a service while it is open.
  • Muting a space clears every member service's badge right away.
  • Fixed a rare launch crash that could happen after a previous session was interrupted while deleting.

Changed

  • Chorus stops retrying a service's icon on every launch when it can't be found, and keeps working if one catalog entry is malformed.

New

  • Rail layouts. In Settings > General, keep the spaces and services rails on the left, stack them on top, or use a hybrid with spaces on the left and service tabs across the top.
  • Bundled brand icons. Each catalog service shows its real logo instead of a scraped favicon.
  • Appearance setting. Follow System, Always Light, or Always Dark for the whole app.

Changed

  • Links that leave a service open in your default browser. A link to another service you keep in Chorus opens there instead, and same-service navigation stays in the app.
  • Opening a Slack workspace now loads in the app instead of a separate window.
  • Per-service dark mode is a single "Force dark mode" checkbox, for services with no dark theme of their own.
  • The back, forward, reload, and home buttons moved to the top-right of the window, and the address bar is gone.

Fixed

  • A Google Docs link opened from Gmail opens in your browser instead of loading inside Gmail.
  • LinkedIn's icons stay visible in forced dark mode, and the empty strip at the bottom-right of its messaging view is gone.
  • Gmail no longer gets stuck with its top bar scrolled out of reach after you switch to it.
  • Workspace chips again show the combined unread count of the services inside them.
  • Accessibility: VoiceOver labels on icon-only controls, higher-contrast badges and offline banner, and quick-switcher text that follows the system text size.

Removed

  • The custom-CSS preset menu. The Custom CSS field and the built-in LinkedIn layout stay.
